question_stem: What number comes next in the sequence? 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ___
explanation: Differences between terms: 3,5,7,9 — each increasing by 2. Next difference is 11. So 26+11 = 37.

question_stem: Pen is to Writer as Scalpel is to ___
explanation: A pen is the primary tool of a writer. A scalpel is the primary surgical tool of a Surgeon.

question_stem: A bicycle shop has 8 bicycles and 5 tricycles. Total number of wheels in the shop is ___
explanation: Bicycles have 2 wheels each: 8x2=16. Tricycles have 3 wheels each: 5x3=15. Total: 16+15=31.

question_stem: Which word is the odd one out: Rose, Tulip, Mango, Sunflower, Lotus
explanation: Mango is a fruit. All others are flowers. This tests classification and categorical thinking.

question_stem: If APPLE is written backwards as ELPPA then how would MANGO be written?
explanation: Reversing letters: M-A-N-G-O reversed is O-G-N-A-M.

question_stem: Priya has Rs.60 and Arjun has Rs.40. They need Rs.120 to buy a gift together. How much more money do they need?
explanation: Priya+Arjun = 60+40 = Rs.100. They need Rs.120. Shortfall = 120-100 = Rs.20.

question_stem: In a row of 10 students Riya stands 4th from the left. How many students are standing to her RIGHT?
explanation: Total students = 10. Riya is at position 4. Students to her right = 10-4 = 6.

question_stem: A shop sells 3 books for Rs.90. The cost of 7 books at the same rate is Rs.___
explanation: Price per book = 90÷3 = Rs.30. Cost of 7 books = 7×30 = Rs.210.

question_stem: What comes next in this pattern? ABAB BCBC CDCD ___
explanation: Each block shifts both letters forward by one position in the alphabet. After CD comes DE so the pattern is DEDE.

question_stem: Asha can complete a task in 6 days. Bina can complete the same task in 3 days. Working together they finish in:
explanation: Asha's rate = 1/6 per day. Bina's rate = 1/3 per day. Combined = 1/6+2/6 = 3/6 = 1/2 per day. So they finish in 2 days.

question_stem: Your class is putting on a school play. You have 30 minutes and 5 tasks. You can only complete 3. Which would you prioritize?
option_a: Costume stage and music (appearance matters most)
option_b: Script costume and stage (story and presence are essential)
option_c: Script lights and stage (core performance elements)
option_d: Music lights and costume (atmosphere matters most)
explanation: Script (content) + lights (visibility) + stage (structure) are the three non-negotiable elements for any performance. Shows priority thinking.

question_stem: You find Rs.500 lying on the school playground. What is the MOST responsible action?
option_b: Hand it immediately to your class teacher
option_c: Share it equally with your friends
explanation: Reporting found money to authority is the ethical and responsible action. Tests integrity and rule-following under low-stakes conditions.

question_stem: Your best friend asks to copy your homework. You would most likely:
option_a: Let her copy — she is your best friend
option_b: Refuse completely and explain why it is not fair to either of you
option_c: Give her a hint so she can try to figure it out herself
option_d: Report it to the teacher right away
explanation: Option C balances empathy with ethics — helping without enabling dishonesty. Signals strong social and ethical reasoning.

question_stem: Your school faces a water shortage every summer. The BEST sustainable solution you could propose would be:
option_a: Ask all students to bring water bottles from home
option_b: Install a rainwater harvesting system on the school roof
option_c: Remind students to use less water every day
option_d: Order packaged bottled water for the school
explanation: Rainwater harvesting is a scalable sustainable infrastructure solution. It addresses root cause not symptoms. Strong signal for engineering and green careers.

question_stem: You are building a model bridge with 10 popsicle sticks and glue that must hold a 200g weight. What would you do FIRST?
option_a: Glue all sticks together and test immediately
option_b: Sketch a plan and figure out the strongest shape before building
option_c: Search online for a bridge design and copy it exactly
option_d: Put the most sticks on top of the bridge
explanation: Planning before executing is the hallmark of engineering thinking. Triangular/arch shapes are strongest — testing shape logic before building is Design Thinking.

question_stem: A new student joins your class and sits alone at lunch every day. What would you do?
option_a: Ignore them — they will make friends eventually
option_b: Invite them to join your group today
option_c: Wait for the teacher to introduce them to classmates
option_d: Tell a few friends to be nicer to the new student
explanation: Direct proactive empathy (B) is the strongest social-emotional intelligence signal. Associated with healthcare counseling social work and team leadership.

question_stem: You want to save Rs.1500 to buy an anniversary gift for your parents in 3 months. You get Rs.200/week pocket money. Your plan would be:
option_a: Stop all snacks and save the full Rs.200/week
option_b: Ask your parents for extra money
option_c: Save Rs.125 per week for 12 weeks to reach exactly Rs.1500
option_d: Buy the gift now and pay back gradually
explanation: Option C shows structured financial planning: 125 x 12 weeks = Rs.1500. Exactly on target. Signals numerical reasoning and goal-directed behavior.

question_stem: Your class project is to design an eco-friendly school bag. The BEST material choice would be:
option_b: Recycled jute or cotton fabric (biodegradable and durable)
explanation: Jute/cotton is biodegradable renewable and culturally relevant in India. Tests environmental awareness and design thinking combined.

question_stem: Rank these activities from 1 (MOST enjoyable) to 5 (LEAST enjoyable) for you personally:
option_b: Solving a tricky math or logic puzzle
option_c: Helping a classmate who is struggling
option_d: Building something with your hands (model or craft)
explanation: Top-ranked activity reveals dominant career interest cluster. Rank 1 = primary cluster signal. Ranks 2-3 = secondary signals.

question_stem: I love learning about how machines and gadgets work — I often take things apart just to see how they are made
explanation: High score strongly predicts engineering mechanics robotics and manufacturing careers. Classic tinkerer profile.

question_stem: I enjoy reading about nature animals or how the human body works — it genuinely excites me
explanation: High score signals biology health sciences veterinary or environmental science cluster.

question_stem: I love activities where I can draw design decorate or create something completely from scratch
explanation: Creative making orientation correlates with design architecture UI/UX media and content creation careers.

question_stem: Sort each into: Would LOVE as a career / Fun HOBBY only / Not interested at all:
option_e: Writing for a school or news publication
explanation: Career bucket gets 3x weight over hobby. Not-interested eliminates clusters. This distinguishes genuine interest from casual enjoyment.

question_stem: I get excited when my group faces a tough challenge and I am the one who figures out the solution
explanation: Problem-solving leadership orientation predicts engineering product management and entrepreneurship tracks.

question_stem: I prefer listening to someone who is upset and helping them feel better over solving a math problem
explanation: Strong empathy orientation (4-5) aligns with medicine nursing psychology counseling and social work careers.

question_stem: Which subjects do you ENJOY learning the most? Rank 1 (most enjoyable) to 5 (least):
explanation: Subject preference at age 13-14 is one of the strongest early predictors of career domain alignment.

question_stem: I believe it is more important to help others than to become famous or very rich
explanation: Social impact values at this age predict long-term satisfaction in medicine research teaching and sustainability careers.

question_stem: What would make you MOST proud of yourself as an adult? Rank 1 (most) to 4 (least):
option_a: Inventing something that millions of people use every day
option_b: Curing a disease that harms children
option_c: Starting a business that employs 500 people
option_d: Winning an Olympic gold medal for India
explanation: Life aspiration at Grade 8 is a powerful intrinsic motivator signal. Maps to core career cluster before external pressure sets in.

question_stem: I get very upset when something is unfair or when rules are broken — I feel strongly about doing the right thing
explanation: Strong justice/fairness orientation is the foundational trait of lawyers judges social workers and policy-makers.

question_stem: I prefer doing things in a completely new or creative way rather than following the usual method everyone uses
explanation: Innovation orientation correlates with design tech entrepreneurship research and creative fields.

question_stem: You worked very hard on a group project and got an A+. But your friend who barely contributed gets equal credit. You would:
option_a: Feel angry and complain to the teacher
option_b: Accept it quietly and move on — it is a team project
option_c: Calmly talk to your friend later about sharing work fairly next time
option_d: Refuse to work in groups for the rest of the year
explanation: Option C shows diplomatic assertiveness — a key leadership and management skill. Option A shows competitive drive. Neither is wrong — each reveals a values signature.

question_stem: Making a difference in the world matters more to me than earning a very high salary
explanation: Impact-before-money orientation at Grade 8 predicts sustained motivation in long demanding careers like medicine research and social enterprise.

question_stem: How do you BEST understand a difficult concept? Select ALL that apply:
option_a: Draw a diagram or mind map
option_b: Read and re-read the textbook carefully
option_c: Watch a video explanation on YouTube or similar
option_d: Try a hands-on activity or simple experiment
option_e: Create practice questions or teach it to someone else
explanation: Learning modality reveals how a student processes information. E (teaching others) is the highest mastery signal. D predicts engineering fit.

question_stem: When I am curious about something I actively look it up and explore beyond what my teacher explained
explanation: Self-directed curiosity is the #1 predictor of success in research academic and advanced STEM careers. It compounds over years.

question_stem: I prefer getting things exactly right even if it takes longer rather than finishing quickly with mistakes
explanation: Detail orientation (4-5) aligns with medicine surgery research quality assurance. Speed orientation (1-2) aligns with entrepreneurship business.

question_stem: Your teacher gives a free project on any topic of your choice. You would:
option_a: Pick the easiest topic to finish quickly
option_b: Ask your parents what would be a good topic
option_c: Choose something you have been genuinely curious about for a long time
option_d: Pick the same topic your popular classmate chose
explanation: Intrinsic motivation (C) is the strongest predictor of long-term academic success. It indicates genuine curiosity not externally driven performance.

question_stem: I remember things better when I connect new information to something I already know or experienced
explanation: Connected learning (relating new to old) is the cognitive style of researchers scientists and strong conceptual thinkers.

question_stem: I enjoy reading books or articles outside of school just because the topic interests me — not because I have to
explanation: Voluntary reading is a cross-domain success predictor. Students who read by choice consistently outperform peers across all career tracks.

question_stem: Which of the following is the BEST example of Artificial Intelligence being used in daily life?
option_b: A streaming app suggesting movies and shows you might like
option_d: A fan automatically cooling the room
explanation: Recommendation systems use machine learning to analyze your past behavior and predict what you will enjoy next. This is core AI — not simple rule-following.

question_stem: True or False: A robot that only follows fixed pre-programmed instructions without learning anything new is considered an AI
explanation: FALSE — True AI learns adapts and improves from data and experience. A robot following fixed rules is automation not artificial intelligence.

question_stem: AI can recognize your face in photos and videos. The technology that enables this is called:
explanation: Computer Vision is the AI field that enables machines to see and interpret visual information from images and video.

question_stem: Your school wants to build a smart attendance system. Which solution actually uses AI?
option_a: A teacher calls roll call and ticks manually
option_b: A fingerprint scanner linked to a database
option_c: A camera that automatically recognizes each student's face and marks them present
option_d: An app where students tap a button to mark attendance
explanation: Face recognition attendance uses Computer Vision and Machine Learning — it learns to identify faces and improves with more data. That is AI in action.

question_stem: Which of these professionals would you MOST likely find working inside a hospital?
explanation: Orthopaedic Surgeons specialize in bones joints and the musculoskeletal system. They work in hospitals clinics and operation theatres.

question_stem: Srishti is 13 years old and absolutely loves animals. She wants a career where she can work with animals every day. The BEST career path for her to explore would be:
explanation: Veterinary Science combines love of animals with medical science. Other animal-related careers: Wildlife Biologist Marine Biologist Zoo Curator.

question_stem: A Data Scientist primarily works with:
option_a: Physical materials and chemicals in a laboratory
option_c: Large amounts of data to find useful patterns and insights
explanation: Data Scientists analyze large datasets using statistics and programming to discover insights that help businesses make decisions.

question_stem: Arjun loves mathematics and enjoys building and fixing things. Which engineering branch would MOST suit him?
explanation: Mechanical engineering focuses on machines motors energy and manufacturing. Civil engineering deals with structures bridges and infrastructure — both require strong math and spatial thinking.

question_stem: Which Indian company is among the world's largest IT services companies?
explanation: Infosys TCS and Wipro are globally ranked IT services companies headquartered in India employing hundreds of thousands of engineers worldwide.

question_stem: True or False: The Sun is the closest star to planet Earth
explanation: TRUE — The Sun is approximately 150 million km from Earth. The next closest star is Proxima Centauri at 4.24 light-years.

question_stem: Which Indian city is called the Silicon Valley of India because of its huge technology industry?
explanation: Bengaluru (Bangalore) hosts thousands of IT companies startups and research labs making it India's technology capital.

question_stem: ISRO stands for:
explanation: ISRO is India's national space agency responsible for Chandrayaan Mangalyaan Gaganyaan and launching 100+ satellites.

question_stem: True or False: Renewable energy sources like solar power and wind energy will never run out because they are continuously replenished by nature
explanation: TRUE — Unlike coal or oil which are finite renewable sources like solar wind hydro and geothermal are naturally replenished and will last as long as the Sun shines.

question_stem: Who is known as the Father of the Indian Space Programme?
explanation: Dr Vikram Sarabhai founded ISRO in 1969 and established India's first rocket launching station at Thumba Kerala.

question_stem: If BOAT is coded as CPBU (each letter shifted forward by 1) then SHIP would be coded as:
explanation: Each letter in SHIP shifts forward by 1: S→T H→I I→J P→Q. Result: TIJQ.

question_stem: In a family: A is B's father. C is A's sister. D is C's mother. How is D related to B?
explanation: D is mother of C. C is sister of A. A is father of B. So D is grandmother of B through the maternal line of A.

question_stem: What is the missing number? 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ___
explanation: These are perfect squares: 1²=1 2²=4 3²=9 4²=16 5²=25 6²=36.

question_stem: Mohit faces South. He turns 90 degrees clockwise then turns 180 degrees. Which direction is he NOW facing?
explanation: Start: South. 90° clockwise → West. 180° turn → East. Final direction: East.

question_stem: Which gas do plants produce during the process of photosynthesis that humans need to breathe?
explanation: Photosynthesis: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + sunlight → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Plants absorb CO₂ and release Oxygen.

question_stem: A rectangle has length 12cm and width 5cm. Its area is ___ square centimetres
explanation: Area of rectangle = length × width = 12 × 5 = 60 cm².

question_stem: The flow of electric charge through a conductor is called:
explanation: Electric Current (measured in Amperes) is the rate of flow of electric charge. Voltage drives it Resistance opposes it.

question_stem: True or False: Sound travels faster through air than through water
explanation: FALSE — Sound travels approximately 1484 m/s through water vs only 343 m/s through air. Denser mediums generally carry sound faster.
